institution.name: North Eastern Mindanao State University
formerly_known_as: Surigao del Sur Polytechnic State College (SSPSC)
institution.address: Rosario, Tandag City, 8300 Surigao del Sur, Philippines

history.summary: NEMSU traces its roots to 1982 when Bukidnon State College opened the Bukidnon External Studies Center (BESC) in Tandag City with 153 pioneering students. After several legislative conversions, it became North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU) on July 30, 2021 through Republic Act 11584.

key_milestones.event: Bukidnon External Studies Center (BESC) opened in Tandag City; 153 pioneering students; supervised by the late Rufina G. Luna, first College Coordinator.

Surigao del Sur Polytechnic College (SSPC) established via Republic Act No. 7377 signed by President Corazon C. Aquino on April 10, 1992. Merged BESC with schools in Cagwait, Tagbina, Lianga, and San Miguel.

Converted to Surigao del Sur Polytechnic State College (SSPSC) through Republic Act 8628 (April 23, 1998). Surigao del Sur Institute of Technology (SSIT) in Cantilan integrated. Dr. Antonio S. Ang became first President.
Dr. Remegita C. Olvida appointed as second President; renewed in 2003 and 2007.
Converted to Surigao del Sur State University (SDSSU) via Republic Act No. 9998 signed February 22, 2010. Dr. Olvida became first University President.
Dr. Baceledes R. Estal appointed University President by the Board of Regents; served two terms until retirement in 2022.
MOA with University of Southeastern Philippines (USEP) led to turnover of USEP Bislig Campus, becoming SDSSU's 7th campus.
Republic Act 11584 signed on July 30, 2021, renaming SDSSU to North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU).

mandate_vision_mission.mandate: The university shall primarily provide advanced education, higher technological, professional instruction and training in trade, fishery, agriculture, science, education, commerce, engineering, forestry, nautical courses and other related fields. It shall also undertake research and extension services and provide progressive leadership in its areas of specialization (RA 9998, Section 2).
mandate_vision_mission.vision: A Research University advancing technology and innovation for sustainable development.
mandate_vision_mission.mission: We drive sustainable development through quality instruction, innovative research, community collaboration, and technological advancement.

core_values.description: Promoting empathy, sincerity, and authenticity within the academic community to foster a caring and supportive environment.
Unwavering commitment to integrity, honesty, and transparent practices ensuring ethical, efficient, and cost-effective stewardship of resources.
Prompt action and consistent quality communication focused on providing correct and complete action/information to clients and stakeholders.
Consistent pursuit of the highest standards of performance characterized by innovation, dedication, and impactful contributions.
Embodiment of professionalism, dedication, and service-oriented mindset committed to fulfilling the university mission with excellence and integrity.

goals.instruction: To develop competitive, resilient and transformative academic community.
goals.research: To produce high impact research and development for the advancement of knowledge, instructional innovation, institutional development, policy formulation, and commercialization.
goals.extension: To improve the lives of the rural and urban poor through transfer of technology and knowledge.
goals.production: To develop and improve resource generation to sustain University operations.
goals.governance: To deliver transparent, responsive and excellent governance for client satisfaction.

campuses.name: Main Campus (Tandag City)
campuses.location: Rosario, Tandag City, 8300 Surigao del Sur
campuses.focus: Graduate Studies, Law, Teacher Education, Arts and Sciences, Business, IT, Engineering
campuses.description: The main administrative and academic hub of NEMSU. Houses the central administration, graduate school, and several undergraduate colleges. Home to the university president's office and all major administrative offices.

campuses.name: Cantilan Campus
campuses.location: Cantilan, Surigao del Sur (117 km south of Surigao City, 67 km north of Tandag City, 3 km from inter-island wharf)
campuses.description: Located along the Pacific coast. Offers over 25 degree programs, with 8 AACCUP-accredited programs. Known for its strong technical-vocational education programs.

campuses.name: San Miguel Campus
campuses.location: Barangay Carromata, Municipality of San Miguel (formerly Tago), Surigao del Sur
campuses.special_identity: Provincial Institute of Agriculture and Forestry (PIAF) under CHED NAFES Program
campuses.description: The biggest NEMSU campus by land area. Located on a scenic plateau overlooking the largest lowland rice bowl of Surigao del Sur Province. Identified by CHED as the Provincial Institute of Agriculture and Forestry (PIAF) under the NAFES program. Caters to the needs of the province and region for skilled agro-forestry workers.

campuses.name: Cagwait Campus
campuses.location: Poblacion, Cagwait, Surigao del Sur (near Cagwait White Beach tourist destination)
campuses.description: Located in the heart of Cagwait, near the famous Cagwait White Beach — a known tourist destination and annual venue of the Kaliguan Festival. Flagship program is BSIT. The BSIT program passed a preliminary survey in 2012 prior to Level I accreditation.

campuses.name: Lianga Campus
campuses.oic_campus_director: Ms. Catherine F. Salomon (Board Secretary V / OIC Campus Director)
campuses.special_identity: National University/College of Fisheries (NUCAF) in CARAGA Region under CHED NAFES Program
campuses.description: Specializes in Fisheries and Marine Sciences. Identified by CHED in September 2009 as the NUCAF in CARAGA Region with a P1M/year grant for 5 years. Notable research expertise in Coastal Resource Management, corals, seaweeds, seagrass, fin fishes, holothurias, and mangroves. Has active fish farming operations and extension programs.
campuses.notable_recognition: Best HEI Extension Program in Caraga Region (2010) - Project Coastal Resource Initiative in Lianga Bay

faq.question: What is NEMSU?
faq.answer: NEMSU stands for North Eastern Mindanao State University. It is a state university located in Tandag City, Surigao del Sur, Philippines. It was renamed from Surigao del Sur State University (SDSSU) through Republic Act 11584 signed on July 30, 2021.

faq.question: Where is the main campus of NEMSU?
faq.answer: The main campus is located in Rosario, Tandag City, 8300 Surigao del Sur, Philippines. Phone: 086-214-4221, Email: information@nemsu.edu.ph.

faq.question: How many campuses does NEMSU have?
faq.answer: NEMSU has 7 campuses: Main Campus (Tandag City), Cantilan Campus, San Miguel Campus, Cagwait Campus, Lianga Campus, Tagbina Campus, and Bislig Campus.

faq.question: Who is the president of NEMSU?
faq.answer: The University President is Dr. Nemesio G. Loayon. He can be reached at op@nemsu.edu.ph or (086) 214-4221.

faq.question: Who is the campus director of Cantilan Campus?
faq.answer: The Campus Director of NEMSU-Cantilan Campus is Dr. Juancho A. Intano. Email: jaintano@nemsu.edu.ph.

faq.question: Who is the campus director of Tagbina Campus?
faq.answer: The Campus Director of NEMSU-Tagbina Campus is Ariston O. Ronquillo, DM. Email: aoronquillo@nemsu.edu.ph.

faq.question: Who is the campus director of Bislig Campus?
faq.answer: The Campus Director of NEMSU-Bislig Campus is For. Whelson C. Pasos. Contact: (086) 647-6452, Email: wcpasos@nemsu.edu.ph.

faq.question: Who is the dean of the College of Business and Management?
faq.answer: The Dean is Prof. Ramel D. Tomaquin. Contact: (086) 214-5195, Email: rdtomaquin@nemsu.edu.ph.

faq.question: Who is the dean of the College of Arts and Sciences?
faq.answer: The Dean is Prof. Romeo A. Patan. Contact: (086) 214-5933, Email: rapatan@nemsu.edu.ph.

faq.question: Who is the dean of the College of Fisheries?
faq.answer: The Dean of the College of Fisheries and Marine Sciences is Dr. Fabio C. Ruaza Jr.

faq.question: Did NEMSU pass the Bar exam?
faq.answer: Yes! NEMSU College of Law achieved a 100% passing rate in both the 2024 and 2025 Bar Examinations. In 2025, their 5-member group, the #AMYzingFive, all passed — and NEMSU ranked 1st among schools with 1-10 examinees.

faq.question: What is the passing rate of NEMSU in the Bar exam?
faq.answer: NEMSU College of Law achieved 100% passing rate in both the 2024 and 2025 Bar Examinations, for two consecutive years.

faq.question: What are the top notcher achievements of NEMSU?
faq.answer: Key achievements include: (1) NEMSU College of Law: 100% Bar Exam passing rate in 2024 and 2025, ranked 1st among schools with 1-10 examinees. (2) NEMSU-Tagbina Campus: 100% passing rate in the August 2025 CHRA Examination, recognized as Top Performing School. For more recent board exam achievements, visit https://www.nemsu.edu.ph/news.

faq.question: What is NEMSU's vision and mission?
faq.answer: Vision: A Research University advancing technology and innovation for sustainable development. Mission: We drive sustainable development through quality instruction, innovative research, community collaboration, and technological advancement.

faq.question: What programs does Cantilan Campus offer?
faq.answer: Cantilan Campus offers graduate and undergraduate programs including BSIT, BS Computer Science, BS Computer Engineering, BTTE programs in multiple majors (Garments, Food, Electrical, Civil, Automotive), BSEd, BSHM, BS Criminology, BSBA, BS Tourism, and BS Industrial Technology in multiple majors.

faq.question: What programs does San Miguel Campus offer?
faq.answer: San Miguel Campus offers BS Forestry, BS Agriculture major in Crop Science, BS Agricultural Technology, and BSEd major in Technology and Livelihood Education.

faq.question: What programs does Cagwait Campus offer?
faq.answer: Cagwait Campus offers BSIT, BS Industrial Technology (Electrical, Culinary, Computer, Automotive), and BS Hospitality Management.

faq.question: What programs does Lianga Campus offer?
faq.answer: Lianga Campus offers BS Marine Biology, BS Fisheries, BS Environmental Science, BS Computer Science, BSBA (Business Economics and Financial Management), BSEd major in Science, BSHM, and BEEd.

faq.question: What programs does Tagbina Campus offer?
faq.answer: Tagbina Campus offers BSEd major in Science, BSHM (Hotel and Restaurant Management), BS Computer Science, BSBA (HRM and Financial Management), BS Agriculture, BEEd, and Bachelor in Agricultural Technology.

faq.question: What programs does Bislig Campus offer?
faq.answer: Bislig Campus offers BTVTE (Automotive and Electrical Technology), BSEd major in English, BS Mechanical Engineering, BS Forestry, and BS Civil Engineering.

faq.question: What are the requirements for new undergraduate students?
faq.answer: New undergraduates need: Form 137 (Original), Good Moral Certificate (Original), Entrance Exam Result, Medical Certificate with Hepa Test Result, Authenticated Birth Certificate (Photocopy), 2x2 ID picture, and a Short Brown Envelope.

faq.question: What are the requirements for transferees?
faq.answer: Transferees need: Original Certificate of Transfer Credentials with TOR (with Evaluation of Grades for temporary enrollment), Good Moral Certificate, Entrance Exam Result, Medical Certificate with Hepa Test, Birth Certificate (Photocopy), 2x2 ID picture, and Short Brown Envelope.

faq.question: What are graduate school admission requirements?
faq.answer: Graduate school applicants need: Certificate of Transfer Credentials with TOR (from other schools) or Photocopy of TOR (for NEMSU graduates), Entrance Exam Result, Birth Certificate (Photocopy), 2x2 ID picture, and a Long Brown Envelope.

faq.question: How do I contact the registrar?
faq.answer: You may contact the Registrar at: registrarmain@nemsu.edu.ph or call +63 (086) 214-5069. The Registrar is Ms. Lynnet A. Sarvida.

faq.question: Does NEMSU have an LMS?
faq.answer: Yes, NEMSU has a Learning Management System (LMS) accessible at https://lms.nemsu.edu.ph.

faq.question: Does NEMSU have online enrollment?
faq.answer: Yes, NEMSU has an online pre-enrollment system called MyPortal at https://preenrollment.nemsu.edu.ph.

faq.question: What are NEMSU's core values?
faq.answer: NEMSU's core values are Compassion, Accountability, Responsiveness, Excellence, and Service — abbreviated as NEMSU CARES.

faq.question: Is NEMSU ISO certified?
faq.answer: Yes, NEMSU is ISO certified.

faq.question: What is NEMSU's mandate?
faq.answer: Under RA 9998 Section 2, NEMSU is mandated to provide advanced education and professional instruction in trade, fishery, agriculture, science, education, commerce, engineering, forestry, nautical courses, and related fields, and to undertake research and extension services.

faq.question: Where can I find downloadable forms?
faq.answer: Downloadable forms are available at https://www.nemsu.edu.ph/downloadables.

faq.question: What research journal does NEMSU publish?
faq.answer: NEMSU publishes the SDSSU Multidisciplinary Research Journal (SMRJ) at https://smrj.nemsu.edu.ph/, edited by Dr. Roxan E. Caray.
